title: festuca pumila subsp. sardoa
intro: Festuca pumila subsp. sardoa, also known as Sardinian blue grass or dwarf blue fescue, is a subalpine grass species native to the Mediterranean region, particularly in the island of Sardinia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Festuca pumila subsp. sardoa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues.
